summary: A rally in technology stocks is lifting US index futures as investors wait for the monthly jobs report. Oil fluctuated while the Trump administration pushed for a peace deal with Iran.

title_cn: 美国贸易法庭裁定特朗普最新 10% 的全球关税非法
summary: US President Donald Trump’s 10% global tariffs were declared unlawful by a federal trade court just months after the US Supreme Court vacated earlier levies he’d imposed. Brendan Murray has the details on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: German Industrial Output Unexpectedly Falls for Second Month
title_cn: 德国工业产出连续第二个月意外下降
summary: German industrial production unexpectedly fell in March for a second month, adding to worries for Europe’s largest economy as it grapples with the Iran war.

title: Pakistan Plans Debut Panda Bond Sale of Up to 1.75 Billion Yuan
title_cn: 巴基斯坦计划首次发行高达17.5亿元人民币的熊猫债
summary: Pakistan is looking to raise yuan-denominated bonds in China’s onshore market, in what would mark its debut panda bond issuance, with a size of as much as 1.75 billion yuan ($257 million).

title: Ex-Hedge Fund Adviser Bets on AI-Backed Consultancy for Rich
title_cn: 前对冲基金顾问押注于人工智能支持的富人咨询服务
summary: When Tony Cowell retired in 2023 from a three-decade career advising private equity and hedge funds from the Cayman Islands at KPMG LLP, AI was still relatively nascent.

title: Food Prices Rise to Highest in Three Years on Iran War Costs
title_cn: 伊朗战争造成粮食价格升至三年来最高
summary: Global food prices climbed to their highest level in more than three years as the Iran war disrupted supply chains, raising the prospect of larger bills for shoppers.

title: Clariant CEO: Demand Remains Solid Despite Hormuz Blockade
title_cn: 科莱恩首席执行官：尽管霍尔木兹封锁，需求仍然强劲
summary: Demand for chemical products remains high, even though the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has complicated supply chains, says Conrad Keijzer, CEO of Swiss chemicals company Clariant. He spoke on "The Pulse with Francine Lacqua" on Bloomberg TV.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK Bonds Rally as Starmer Says He’ll Stay as Prime Minister
title_cn: 斯塔默表示将继续担任首相，英国债券上涨
summary: UK government bonds rallied as Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he would stay as the Labour leader despite the party’s election losses.

title: BofA Didn’t Have Block Trade Mandate at Time of Segantii Calls
title_cn: 美国银行在 Segantii 电话会议时没有大宗交易授权
summary: Bank of America Corp. didn’t have a mandate to work on a block trade in Esprit Holdings Ltd. when it had phone calls with Segantii Capital Management to discuss the deal, a prosecution witness said during the hedge fund’s trial for insider trading.

title: Gold Fields’ Spat With Ghana Contractor Heads for Arbitration
title_cn: 金田公司与加纳承包商的争执即将提交仲裁
summary: A $740 million dispute between Gold Fields Ltd. and a mining contractor in Ghana will be fought out in arbitration.

title: FSD Africa to Raise $400 Million SME Financing for Five Nations
title_cn: FSD Africa 将为五个国家筹集 4 亿美元中小企业融资
summary: FSD Africa, an agency backed by the UK’s Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office, is establishing funds to raise as much as $400 million of capital for small businesses in five nations.

title: BofA’s Hartnett Says US Stock Gains Have Rarely Been This Strong
title_cn: 美国银行的哈特内特表示，美国股市涨幅很少如此强劲
summary: US stocks and gold are hurtling toward a fourth year of double-digit gains, rallies so exceptional that there have been few such episodes in history, according to Bank of America Corp. strategists.

title: Oil’s Price Surge Spurs Nigeria’s Flip From Discount to Darling
title_cn: 石油价格飙升促使尼日利亚从折扣转向亲爱的
summary: Nigerian assets are rallying across stocks, bonds and the currency as investor confidence builds in President Bola Tinubu’s economic agenda.

title: Rheinmetall Loses Bull in JPMorgan After Slew of Results Misses
title_cn: 莱茵金属公司在一系列业绩失误后失去了对摩根大通的看好
summary: Rheinmetall AG’s position as the standout European defense stock is looking increasingly vulnerable after a series of disappointing results led a long-time bull to downgrade the shares.

title: DBS Sells Private Credit Fund Yielding Up to 20% to Rich Clients
title_cn: 星展银行向富有客户出售收益率高达 20% 的私人信贷基金
summary: Singapore-based firm Granite Asia has engaged the private banking arm of DBS Group Holding Ltd. to raise additional capital for its first private credit vehicle, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Investors Are Looking Through the Conflict, Goldman Sachs Says
title_cn: 高盛表示，投资者正在审视这场冲突
summary: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, managing director for portfolio strategy and asset allocation at Goldman Sachs, discusses risk appetite amid US efforts to end the war in Iran. "A lot of the clients we speak to do very little in terms of taking a view on that conflict," Mueller-Glissmann tells Bloomberg Television. "They rather try to do tweaks and twists to the portfolio and focus on diversification." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Rheinmetall CEO Sees High Mideast Demand for Air Defense Systems
title_cn: 莱茵金属首席执行官看到中东地区对防空系统的巨大需求
summary: Rheinmetall AG sees growing demand for its air defense systems in the Middle East amid the Iran conflict and hopes to nearly double the number in use there by the end of 2027, the company’s chief executive officer said in an interview.

title: Stellantis Deal Gives China’s Leapmotor Plant, EV in Europe
title_cn: Stellantis交易让中国零跑汽车工厂在欧洲建立电动汽车工厂
summary: Stellantis NV is giving China’s Leapmotor access to a plant in Spain, and the partners will build an EV together to deepen ties as the maker of Opel and Fiat cars seeks to shore up its European operations.

title: Ghana Targets $1 Billion in Cocoa Bonds as Part of Overhaul
title_cn: 加纳目标发行 10 亿美元可可债券作为改革的一部分
summary: Ghana is planning to raise $1 billion through domestic bonds to fund cocoa purchases from farmers, part of an overhaul of the way it delivers the commodity to global buyers.

title: Novo Nordisk’s Ozempic India Price Cut Boosts Obesity Drug Sales
title_cn: 诺和诺德 Ozempic India 降价促进肥胖药物销售
summary: Novo Nordisk A/S saw a 40% surge in sales of its diabetes and weight-loss drugs in India last month, after steep price cuts boosted demand for its branded treatments even as generic GLP-1 therapies flood the market.

title: Mariana Mazzucato Thinks We Need More Moonshots | Odd Lots
title_cn: Mariana Mazzucato 认为我们需要更多登月计划零股
summary: Today's guest Mariana Mazzucato is one of our most requested. Mazzucato, who is the director of the UCL Institute for Innovation and Public Purpose, specializes in the political economy of technological development and public sector investment. In our conversation, recorded in Madrid while we are at the Bloomberg CityLab conference, she explains her concept of the "mission economy," her definition of state capacity, how to prevent top talent from fleeing to the private sector, and whether consultants or governments should be blamed for inefficiencies and civic failures. It's a wide-ranging interview, one that covers everything from the initial public financing of Silicon Valley algorithms to the history of moonshots. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Global Central Banks Boost Their Holdings of Malaysia Bonds
title_cn: 全球央行增持马来西亚债券
summary: Foreign central banks and governments have boosted their share of Malaysian sovereign bonds, underscoring the nation’s growing appeal as a reserve asset in a volatile global environment.

title: Bulgaria Lawmakers Approve Radev’s Cabinet After Landslide Win
title_cn: 保加利亚立法者在压倒性胜利后批准拉德夫内阁
summary: Bulgaria’s parliament voted in the nation’s first single-party government with a majority this century after Rumen Radev’s landslide victory, ushering in another European Union leader critical of the bloc’s mainstream.

title: South East Water CEO Quits After Pressure From UK Lawmakers
title_cn: 东南水务首席执行官在英国立法者的压力下辞职
summary: The embattled chief executive officer of the UK’s South East Water resigned after a campaign by lawmakers and customers in the wake of multiple outages across Kent that affected tens of thousands of people.

title: M&G’s Fedeli Shifts to Cheaper Stocks After Tech’s Rapid Rally
title_cn: 科技股快速上涨后，M&G 的 Fedeli 转向更便宜的股票
summary: M&G Investment Management is taking profit in technology stocks that have seen rapid gains and is instead seeking undervalued companies with strong fundamentals given volatile markets, said executive Fabiana Fedeli.

title: Trump Wants to Make H-1B Workers More Expensive for US Employers
title_cn: 特朗普希望让 H-1B 工人对美国雇主来说更加昂贵
summary: An entry‑level software engineer in San Francisco would need to be paid $162,000 a year to qualify for an H‑1B visa under a Trump administration proposal — almost 30% more than today. In Dallas, the minimum would jump by a similar rate to $113,000 and in New York to $132,000.

title: Cloudflare Drops After Sales Forecast Miss, AI-Linked Job Cuts
title_cn: Cloudflare 在销售预测未达标、与 AI 相关的裁员之后下降
summary: Cloudflare Inc. stock fell in premarket trading on Friday after the company said it would slash jobs and gave a forecast for revenue that fell short of analysts’ expectations.

title: Coinbase Resumes Trading After Seven-Hour Exchange Outage
title_cn: Coinbase 在交易所中断七小时后恢复交易
summary: Crypto exchange Coinbase Global Inc. said trading services had resumed after a significant outage left customers unable to transact on its platform for almost seven hours on Friday, rounding off a difficult week at the company.

title: Intel CEO Who Won Over Trump and Musk Now Needs a Breakthrough
title_cn: 赢得特朗普和马斯克支持的英特尔首席执行官现在需要突破
summary: After Lip-Bu Tan became chief executive officer of Intel Corp. in March of last year, the struggling company’s shares went nowhere for seven months while the chipmaker was getting trounced in the market for artificial intelligence.

title: SoftBank Cuts Target for OpenAI Margin Loan by 40%
title_cn: 软银将 OpenAI 保证金贷款目标下调 40%
summary: SoftBank is scaling back plans for a $10 billion margin loan backed by its OpenAI stake after creditors voiced some hesitation. Matthew Bloxham of Bloomberg Intelligence has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Baidu’s Chip Unit Plans Dual IPO in Shanghai and Hong Kong
title_cn: 百度芯片部门计划在上海和香港双重IPO
summary: Baidu Inc.’s chip unit Kunlunxin is planning an initial public offering on Shanghai’s Nasdaq-style bourse in addition to a separate listing plan in Hong Kong as the Chinese search engine giant looks to taps investor appetite for semiconductor stocks.

title: Waymo vs Wayve: The Self-Driving Showdown Coming to London
title_cn: Waymo vs Wayve：自动驾驶对决即将来到伦敦
summary:
Waymo leads with sensors and maps. Wayve bets on pure AI and scale. Both are launching in London—who wins the future of driving? 
Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie reports (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Autonomous Driving Showdown: Who Will Win the Self-Driving Race? | Bloomberg Tech: Europe 5/08/2026
title_cn: 自动驾驶对决：谁将赢得自动驾驶竞赛？ |彭博科技：欧洲 2026 年 5 月 8 日
summary:
In this episode of Bloomberg Tech: Europe, Bloomberg’s Tom Mackenzie explores the accelerating race for autonomous driving. From Waymo’s sensor-driven precision to Wayve’s mapless, end-to-end AI approach, the battle for the future of mobility is intensifying. Which model will win out? We also hear from BYD, examine Vay’s vision for driverless transport, and see how Einride is reshaping freight with autonomous, electric trucks.  Who will lead the driverless future?

"Bloomberg Tech: Europe" spotlights the biggest names and trends shaping the region's technology ecosystem as the global competition heats up. This monthly, 30-minute magazine-style show features in-depth interviews with top technology leaders, as well as major investors and policymakers - giving you a compelling A to Z of the most consequential innovations, opportunities and challenges.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sony Partners With TSMC in Effort to Rein in Chipmaking Costs
title_cn: 索尼与台积电合作，努力控制芯片制造成本
summary: Sony Group Corp. plans a new joint venture with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. as the first step toward what it called a fab-light approach to semiconductor production.

title: Hackers Bring Down Service Used by Colleges From Harvard to Oslo
title_cn: 黑客破坏了从哈佛到奥斯陆等大学使用的服务
summary: Hackers briefly took down an online portal used by thousands of colleges around the world this month, disrupting services at institutions from Harvard to Princeton.

title: Freight's Future Is Electric and Autonomous, Einride CEO Says
title_cn: Einride 首席执行官表示，货运的未来是电动和自动化
summary: Roozbeh Charli, chief executive officer of Swedish firm Einride, discusses how platform-driven logistics, electric trucks, and autonomous systems are transforming global freight, and why humans still play a critical role. He speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Said to Suspect Nvidia Chips Smuggled to Alibaba Via Thailand
title_cn: 美国怀疑英伟达芯片经泰国走私至阿里巴巴
summary: A key company behind Thailand’s national AI effort is suspected of helping to smuggle billions of dollars worth of Super Micro Computer Inc. servers containing advanced Nvidia Corp. chips to China, with Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. one of multiple end customers, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Wayve CEO on Tesla, Waymo, Future of Self-Driving Cars
title_cn: Wayve 首席执行官谈特斯拉、Waymo、自动驾驶汽车的未来
summary: Wayve CEO Alex Kendall explains end-to-end autonomous driving, how Wayve's approach differs to those of Tesla and Waymo, and why artificial intelligence licensing could scale fastest. He speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: AI Is Reshaping Self-Driving Cars, Wayve CEO Says
title_cn: Wayve 首席执行官表示，人工智能正在重塑自动驾驶汽车
summary: Wayve CEO Alex Kendall explains how an artificial intelligence-driven approach to self-driving -- using onboard intelligence and real-world learning -- is reshaping how autonomous cars and robotaxis are built and scaled. Kendall speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How Waymo Builds Self-Driving Cars
title_cn: Waymo 如何制造自动驾驶汽车
summary: Srikanth Thirumalai, vice president of onboard software at Waymo, breaks down the company's approach to autonomous cars. He speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: How Waymo's Driverless Strategy Compares to Tesla's
title_cn: Waymo 的无人驾驶战略与特斯拉的相比如何
summary: Srikanth Thirumalai, vice president of onboard software at Waymo, explains the company's full-stack approach drive simulator, its safety-critique, and how it stacks up against Tesla's strategy. He speaks to Bloomberg's Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nintendo Plans Switch 2 Price Increase as Outlook Disappoints
title_cn: 由于前景令人失望，任天堂计划提高 Switch 2 的价格
summary: Nintendo Co. said it will increase the price of its Switch 2 console to $500 from $450, acknowledging pressure on profitability of its flagship device heading into its second year on the market.

title: Earnings Resilience Amid Iran Risk: Markets Snapshot
title_cn: 伊朗风险中的盈利弹性：市场快照
summary: The shape-shifting artificial intelligence trade and the ongoing conflict in the Middle East dominated conversations in a week that saw strong earnings results. The Opening Trade spoke with leading market voices and company executives to get their take. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK’s Intertek Spurns EQT’s Latest Bid Worth £8.9 Billion
title_cn: 英国 Intertek 拒绝了 EQT 89 亿英镑的最新报价
summary: Intertek Group Plc rejected the latest takeover offer from EQT AB as the product testing business continues to resist the private equity firm’s advances.

title: SoftBank Cuts Target for OpenAI Margin Loan by 40% to $6 Billion
title_cn: 软银将 OpenAI 保证金贷款目标下调 40% 至 60 亿美元
summary: SoftBank Group Corp. has downsized plans for a $10 billion margin loan backed by its OpenAI stake after facing hesitation from some creditors, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Invesco's Ivanova Sees Credit Opportunities in Software
title_cn: Invesco 的 Ivanova 看到了软件领域的信贷机会
summary: Alexandra Ivanova, fund manager at Invesco Asset Management, discusses the outlook for credit markets and where she sees opportunities. "Software has been one of the big underperformers," Ivanova tells Bloomberg Television. "There are places there that I think there are resilient businesses." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: TSMC’s Sales Grow Slowest in Months Even as AI Buildout Persists
title_cn: 尽管人工智能持续扩张，但台积电销售额增长数月来最慢
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. posted its slowest pace of monthly revenue expansion since October, highlighting the potential challenges of sustaining a torrid AI-fueled pace of growth.

title: Airtel Delays IPO of Mobile Money Unit to Second Half of 2026
title_cn: Airtel 将移动货币部门的首次公开募股推迟至 2026 年下半年
summary: Airtel Africa Plc said it plans an initial public offering of its mobile money business in the second half of this year, delaying the sale by a few months.

title: Xero Founder Returns New Zealand Prize After Misconduct Claims
title_cn: Xero 创始人在不当行为索赔后归还新西兰奖金
summary: Rod Drury, the founder of ASX-listed accounting software firm Xero, has handed back the New Zealander of the Year prize he won just weeks ago following allegations of sexual misconduct involving former employees.

title: US Rare Earths Champion Views Iran War as Demand ‘Accelerant’
title_cn: 美国稀土冠军将伊朗战争视为需求“催化剂”
summary: The conflict in the Middle East has highlighted how future wars will be underpinned by rare-earth magnets that are vital in drones and robots, according to MP Materials Corp., the Pentagon-backed producer.

title: Citi Targets Hedge Funds, Private Equity in FX Growth Push
title_cn: 花旗瞄准对冲基金和私募股权推动外汇增长
summary: Citigroup Inc. is expanding its foreign-exchange business with hedge fund and private-equity clients, part of a broader push as global trading volumes rise.

title: South Korea’s Current Account Surplus Surges to Record on Chips
title_cn: 韩国经常账户顺差飙升至创纪录水平
summary: South Korea’s current-account surplus surged to another record in March, driven by a surge in semiconductor exports and stronger shipments across Asia and the US, even as foreign investors sharply reduced holdings of domestic stocks amid rising Middle East tensions.

title: No AI, Poor Returns Drive Indian Investors to Foreign Markets
title_cn: 没有人工智能，回报不佳促使印度投资者转向海外市场
summary: Investors in India, long focused almost entirely on domestic markets, are increasingly starting to look outward.

title: How the Strait of Hormuz Has Become a Weapon of War
title_cn: 霍尔木兹海峡如何成为战争武器
summary: No region of the world produces more oil and gas than the countries straddling the Persian Gulf. Most of this energy can only be exported aboard tankers that cross the Strait of Hormuz — a waterway that’s effectively been blocked for more than two months.

title: Australia Sees Military Allies Ready to Work Together If Needed
title_cn: 澳大利亚认为军事盟友已准备好在需要时合作
summary: Australia’s military is ready to deploy with its allies if needed in Asia or beyond, its defense forces chief said Friday at the conclusion of military drills in the South China Sea with the US, Philippines and others.

title: Israel Tightens Grip on Gaza as Deadlock With Hamas Persists
title_cn: 以色列加强对加沙的控制，与哈马斯的僵局持续存在
summary: Israel has expanded its control of Gazan territory and is considering more intense military action, further squeezing the war-torn enclave.

title: London Vote Offers Snapshot of Fractured and Fractious UK
title_cn: 伦敦公投展现了英国支离破碎、暴躁的一面
summary: The map of English councils was lighting up in a murky gray on Friday morning, as more and more town halls returned a divided composition that spells future budget paralysis.

title: Festus Mogae, Who Led Botswana for a Decade, Has Died at 86
title_cn: 领导博茨瓦纳十年的费斯图斯·莫加去世，享年 86 岁
summary: Festus Mogae, an Oxford-educated economist who served as president of Botswana for a decade before stepping down, has died. He was 86.

title: Ukraine Ramps Up Drone Strikes Ahead of Moscow’s Parade
title_cn: 乌克兰在莫斯科阅兵前加强无人机袭击
summary: Ukraine conducted heavy overnight drone attacks on Russia, giving no indication that Kyiv is ready to observe Moscow’s self-declared ceasefire despite threats of retaliation if Victory Day celebrations on Red Square are disrupted.

title: Merz’s Popularity Plunges to Record Low for a German Chancellor
title_cn: 德国总理默茨的支持率跌至历史新低
summary: Friedrich Merz’s popularity has hit a historic low for German chancellors in a new poll, underscoring his struggle to convince voters he’s the person to lead the European Union’s largest economy.

title: Iranian Army Detains Oil Tanker for ‘Violations,’ Tasnim Reports
title_cn: 塔斯尼姆报道称，伊朗军队因“违规”而扣押油轮
summary: The Iranian army seized an oil tanker for “attempting to disrupt oil exports and the interests of the Iranian nation,” the semi-official Tasnim news agency reported.

title: Starmer Vows to Stay On as Farage Gains in Labour Heartlands
title_cn: 斯塔默誓言随着工党中心地带的票价上涨而继续留任
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he had no plans to step aside as Labour leader after early results in local elections showed Nigel Farage’s populist Reform UK racking up sweeping gains over Britain’s governing party.

title: The Power Needs of AI Are Becoming a Crunch Issue for Governments
title_cn: 人工智能的电力需求正在成为政府的一个关键问题
summary: Governments are already conducting probes of AI’s energy use.

title: Russians Weary of War, Repression Start to Blame the Kremlin
title_cn: 俄罗斯人厌倦了战争和镇压，开始指责克里姆林宫
summary: For years, many Russians cheered on Vladimir Putin’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in a wave of nationalist fervor. Others kept silent, fearful of the state cracking down against even mild criticism of the social and economic fallout.

title: Anthony Scaramucci: Wall Street Has Trump Regrets
title_cn: 安东尼·斯卡拉穆奇：华尔街对特朗普感到遗憾
summary: Anthony Scaramucci tells Mishal Husain that Wall Street is having regrets about support for Donald Trump. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: UK Voters Turn Away From Starmer in Local Election
title_cn: 英国选民在地方选举中不再支持斯塔默
summary: Early results in the local elections showed Nigel Farage’s populist Reform UK racking up sweeping gains over Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Labour. The results reaffirm a splintering of British politics that’s seen the insurgent Reform and Green parties make up ground on the right and left. Bloomberg's Alex Morgan reports from London. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Taiwan Passes Key $25 Billion Defense Budget to Deter China
title_cn: 台湾通过250亿美元关键国防预算以威慑中国
summary: Taiwanese lawmakers approved a special defense budget proposed by President Lai Ching-te that is intended to help the democracy build a state-of-the-art system to counter aerial threats from China.

title: Clashes Near Hormuz Send Oil Higher, UK Local Elections | The Opening Trade 5/8/2026
title_cn: 霍尔木兹附近的冲突导致英国地方选举油价上涨开盘交易 5/8/2026
summary:
The US and Iran clashed near the Strait of Hormuz, an escalation that threatens to further fracture a fragile ceasefire as the two sides discuss a permanent end to the war. US forces targeted missile and drone launch sites and other military assets in Iran that they said were responsible for attacking three US warships transiting the strait. No vessels were hit, US Central Command said. A monthlong ceasefire remains in effect, President Donald Trump said.

Nigel Farage’s Reform UK racked up sweeping gains in the first counts in local elections, with British voters continuing to turn away from Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s governing Labour Party.

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Tom Mackenzie. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: What It Would Take for Labour to Replace Keir Starmer
title_cn: 工党需要什么才能取代凯尔·斯塔默
summary: Keir Starmer’s political struggles have left some in his Labour Party contemplating the once-unthinkable and mounting a challenge to replace him as prime minister.

title: South Africa’s Top Court Rekindles Ramaphosa Cash-in-Sofa Saga
title_cn: 南非最高法院重燃拉马福萨沙发现金传奇
summary: South Africa’s top court faulted parliament’s decision to quash a report that fueled efforts to remove President Cyril Ramaphosa from office over his handling of a burglary at his game farm.

title: Honduras Reviews China Deals, Mulls Restoring Taiwan Ties
title_cn: 洪都拉斯审视中国交易，考虑恢复与台湾的关系
summary: Honduras’ new president is reviewing his predecessor’s agreements with China, a process that could help the US push to reduce the Asian nation’s influence in the region and lead to recognition of Taiwan.

title: Iran Turns to China Rail Link to Try to Bypass US Blockade
title_cn: 伊朗转向中国铁路试图绕过美国封锁
summary: Iran is ramping up trade with China via rail in a bid to blunt the impact of a US blockade of its ports and adapt to pressure designed to strangle its economy.

title: Iran War: Clashes Near Strait of Hormuz Threaten Fragile Ceasefire | Daybreak Europe 5/8/2026
title_cn: 伊朗战争：霍尔木兹海峡附近的冲突威胁脆弱的停火|黎明欧洲 5/8/2026
summary:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. And we'll tell you what matters for investors in Europe, giving you insight before trading begins.

On today's show, the US and Iran have clashed near the Strait of Hormuz, threatening the fragile ceasefire. President Trump says the truce is still in effect, and Iran needs to make a deal soon.

The UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer awaits his fate, as early results from local elections paint an ugly picture for his ruling Labour Party.

And Commerzbank has lifted its profit guidance for the year, as it steps up its defence against a hostile takeover attempt by UniCredit.

Today's guests: Robert Hayward, Polling Analyst & UK House of Lords, Conservative Party Member and Maria Demertzis, European University Institute, Economic Policy Professor (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Myanmar’s Neighbors Seek to Ease Its Isolation Despite Conflict
title_cn: 尽管发生冲突，缅甸邻国仍寻求缓解孤立状态
summary: Sentiment is warming among some Southeast Asian nations toward easing Myanmar’s isolation five years after the overthrow of Aung San Suu Kyi, with diplomats signaling a desire to bring the crisis-torn nation back into the fold despite ongoing conflict.

title: Farage’s Reform Racks Up Gains in UK Local Election
title_cn: 法拉奇的改革在英国地方选举中获胜
summary: Nigel Farage’s Reform UK racked up sweeping gains in the first counts in local elections, with British voters continuing to turn away from Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s governing Labour Party. The results show a splintering of British politics, with the insurgent Reform and Green parties making gains. Bloomberg’s Alex Morgan reports from London.

title: Pretty Awful Day For Labour: UK Polling Analyst
title_cn: 英国民意调查分析师：对于工党来说这是非常糟糕的一天
summary: Polling analyst and Conservative Party peer Robert Hayward says early indications following UK local elections show the results are "as bad as people were predicting for Labour", but London so far appears to be the one exception.  Early results suggest a "great night for Reform", according to Hayward. But a full picture of the results might not come into focus until late in the UK day. In an interview on Bloomberg Daybreak Europe, Hayward said it will be a "summer of discontent" for Labour as they argue over whether or not to replace Prime Minister Keir Starmer. He added that he "wouldn't be at all surprised" if this happened over the next few months.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Thailand, Cambodia Seek to Rebuild Ties After Deadly Clashes
title_cn: 泰国和柬埔寨在致命冲突后寻求重建关系
summary: Thailand and Cambodia pledged this week to repair fractured ties, even as a new rift over a canceled maritime pact threatens to undermine a fragile peace following last year’s deadly border clashes.

title: One Year After Pakistan Clash, Trump Stokes Worries in India
title_cn: 巴基斯坦冲突一年后，特朗普引发印度担忧
summary: In the year since India and Pakistan came close to all-out war, officials in New Delhi have become increasingly worried about Islamabad’s cozy relations with US President Donald Trump.

title: US Fires on Targets in Iran, Awaits Response to Proposal
title_cn: 美国向伊朗目标开火，等待对提案的回应
summary: The US struck military targets in Iran after the country fired on three Navy destroyers sailing in the Strait of Hormuz. The latest clash heightens tensions in the region as the US attempts to exit a war now in its third month. President Donald Trump’s administration has been waiting for Iran to respond to its proposal to reopen the strait and end the conflict. Bloomberg’s Stuart Livingstone-Wallace reports.

title: Saudi Arabia Boosts Logistics Spending to Blunt War Impact
title_cn: 沙特阿拉伯增加物流支出以减轻战争影响
summary: Saudi Arabia brought forward some planned spending on infrastructure and food imports to soften the impact of the Iran war and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a move that added to its budget deficit in the first quarter.
